titleStmt.title: The Ambassador in China ( Hurley ) to the Secretary of State

div.head: The Ambassador in China ( Hurley ) to the Secretary of State
div.p: 863. Although the Department will, of course, have been fully informed by our Embassy in Lisbon of the recent Japanese peace feeler, I submit as of probable interest the following summary of a telegram from Chang Chien, Chinese Minister there, which has been made available by the Acting Foreign Minister:K. C. Wu, Chinese Political Vice Minister for Foreign Affairs.
[Here follows summary of telegram similar to telegram 990, May 7, noon, from Lisbon, printed on page 478.]

When handing this message to Briggs,Ellis O. Briggs, Economic Counselor of Embassy in China. Wu stated that the Chinese Foreign Office had already replied, declaring that China will accept nothing less than unconditional surrender of Japan. He also commented upon the “obvious effort” of Japan to sow suspicion of Russia.
